task_completion.description: Open the Programming Maze activity in GCompris
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application is running and the 'Programming Maze' activity is visible, showing Tux the penguin, a maze, and programming command blocks (e.g., Forward, Turn).

task_completion.description: Guide Tux to the goal to complete Level 1
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tux is shown reaching the goal destination in the Level 1 maze, typically accompanied by a success animation, a completed path, or a 'Level complete' indicator.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the Level 1 completion to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool, save dialog, terminal command, or file manager shows an image of the completed Level 1 being saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/programming_maze_complete.png'.

task_completion.description: Load Level 2 of the Programming Maze
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris activity transitions to Level 2, displaying a new, different maze layout with Tux at the starting position.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the Level 2 maze to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool, save dialog, terminal command, or file manager shows an image of the Level 2 maze being saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/programming_maze_level2.png'.

integrity.description: The agent actively played the game using the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show the agent clicking or dragging command blocks (Forward, Turn) within the GCompris interface to solve the maze, rather than bypassing the game logic.

integrity.description: The screenshots are genuine captures of the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved images must clearly depict the GCompris application window running in the current environment, not stock images downloaded from a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not create empty or fake files to pass path checks
integrity.visual_evidence: The files at '/home/ga/Documents/programming_maze_complete.png' and '/home/ga/Documents/programming_maze_level2.png' must be actual image files containing the correct game scenes, not empty files created via terminal commands like 'touch'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Programming Maze is a verified GCompris activity where the user programs Tux (a penguin) to navigate mazes using instructions like 'Forward', 'Turn Left', and 'Turn Right'. The task requires saving two specific screenshots: one showing Level 1 completion at '/home/ga/Documents/programming_maze_complete.png' and one showing the Level 2 maze at '/home/ga/Documents/programming_maze_level2.png'.
